title: Ixora recurva
intro: Ixora recurva, also known as Jungle Geranium or Ixora, is a flowering plant species native to tropical regions, characterized by its vibrant red and purple flowers. The health benefits of Ixora recurva include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ixora recurva include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ixora recurva is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Ixora recurva is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to India and Southeast Asia. The genus Ixora is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the jungle geranium or West Indian jessamine. The Ixora genus is part of the tribe Spermaeae and subfamily Ixoroideae. Ixora recurva is a type of shrub that is often cultivated for its fragrant flowers and attractive foliage.
uses: Ixora recurva is known to have various medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Indian medicine. The plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its leaves and roots are used to prepare medicinal concoctions, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, Ixora recurva is also used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Ixora recurva in treating these conditions.

parts: Ixora recurva has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. The leaves and flowers of the plant are used in Ayurvedic medicine. preparations: Ixora recurva herbal preparations are not well-documented, but some sources suggest that the plant has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world.
